# Platforms Just What Are They?
This week had a lot more to learn about then I had thought mainly the fact how platforms operate. What "Platform-based" means; when you build a platform-bases app your code becomes part of the design. Each platform imposes different rules, constraints, and opportunities. 
Here are some examples of different platforms: 
- Web apps live in the browser, interact with the DOM, and disappear when the tab closes.
- Mobile apps run on devices with cameras, sensors, GPS, and strict OS‑level lifecycle rules.
- Edge functions run on remote servers for milliseconds at a time and may never share memory between runs.

## Why learn multiple platforms:
You might be asking yourself, "Why should I learn more than one platform?" Now let me explain why you should learn more than one:
- 1. The patterns are univeral:
     These are not tied to any one platform and can be used across all forms of platforms. Once you have an understanding      of these patterns you can recognize them everywhere.
- 2. Real apps span over multiple platforms:
     If we take lets say Spotify we notice the one product spans over multiple platforms. Modern software is a series of       interconnected systems. Even if you specialize in one thing you will need to understand how your piece interacts          with the rest.
- 3. The job market rewards platform fluency:
     Now a days you can't say "I only know Android" you have to be able to say that you know more than one platform. You       should have one or two specialties in platforming and enough understanding to be able to manage other platforms even      if simply.
     
## My thoughts on MIT app inventor:
MIT app inventor is a great starting point for beginners. Now it won't teach you how to code effectively but it will give you a better understanding on how code works together with each other. I find that more beneficial in the long run for a new coder, such as myself.

## Summary:
This week of learning for me was informative as it was an intro to thinking like an actual developer who understands platforms. No matter what I use as a platform, as long as I have an understanding of the platform patterns I will be able to succeed. It might take a while to learn but I think I will be able to eventually understand it a lot more than now.
